title: senecio ovatus subsp. alpestris
intro: Senecio ovatus subsp. alpestris, also known as alpine ragwort, is a subspecies of the Senecio ovatus plant, a member of the dandelion family and native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Senecio ovatus subsp. alpestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Senecio ovatus subsp. alpestris typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for internal use.
